summary:
The modified Widman flap minimizes gum recession by limiting wide tissue refection during surgery; it effectively provides necessary exposure without causing significant additional gum loss after treatment. Sentence count: 2
To improve conciseness further, I will refine this summary slightly but maintain its core message about reducing gum recession through specific techniques used in periodontal surgeries.

Revised Summary: 
The modified Widman flap technique limits extensive tissue dissection, thereby reducing gum recession risk when treating gingival recessions surgically. This method ensures adequate exposure with minimal adverse effects on soft tissues.
